title: json_skip_number accepts RFC 8259-invalid leading zeros
description: RFC 8259 §6: a non-zero integer part must not have leading zeros (e.g. 01 is invalid JSON, only 0 or 1 is valid). json_skip_number at secretx-core/src/lib.rs:359 consumes digits greedily without checking. Trigger: secret value is JSON containing a field with value like 01 or 007 in a non-extracted field. Bad behavior: the skip path accepts malformed JSON and continues, potentially extracting a field from a document that should have been rejected. Low practical risk since cloud backends emit valid JSON, but inconsistent with the other RFC 8259 fixes already applied in this round.
